postText:
Most organisations treating AI adoption as a training problem will waste the next 18 months.

@Anthropic just announced the Claude Corps programme. A cohort of experts embedded directly into organisations to accelerate AI deployment. On paper, it sounds like the answer.

Here is what I have watched happen inside a telecoms company with 28,000 employees. The knowledge lands. The pilots run. Then the expert leaves, and the organisation snaps back to exactly where it started. Because the constraint was never understanding. It was accountability.

A facilities management firm sounds completely different from a logistics company when you ask them why AI stalled. But the pattern underneath is identical. Nobody owned what happened after the workshop.

Embedded expertise only compounds if someone internal is accountable for carrying it forward. That person needs authority, not just enthusiasm.

The Claude Corps model could work. It depends entirely on what it leaves behind inside the organisation, not what it delivers during the engagement.

What does your organisation actually do with external expertise once it walks out the door?

#EnterpriseAI #TechLeadership #DigitalTransformation
